Container.Name: windrose-server
Container.Overview: Windrose Dedicated Server — keep your world online 24/7 so your crew can sail, build, and plunder without needing a host. This container is built to actually work: it uses host networking and auto-detects your LAN IP so the P2PGate relay can route players to your server correctly. No port forwarding required. Other containers default P2P_PROXY_ADDRESS to 127.0.0.1 (loopback), which silently breaks connections for anyone outside your local network, and expose little to no configuration in the Unraid UI — requiring manual JSON editing just to set a server name or password. First launch auto-generates all required config files. Set your invite code, server name, password, and player cap via environment variables — no manual JSON editing needed. Auto-updates server files on every start via SteamCMD so you stay in sync with game patches automatically.
Container.Description:
Windrose Dedicated Server — keep your world online 24/7 so your crew can sail, build, and plunder without needing a host.
WHY THIS CONTAINER: Most Windrose Docker containers set P2P_PROXY_ADDRESS to 127.0.0.1 (loopback) by default. This silently breaks P2P connections for anyone outside your local network — players on the same LAN appear to connect, but nobody on the internet can join. This container uses host networking and auto-detects your LAN IP at startup so the Windrose P2PGate relay can route players to your server correctly. No port forwarding required.
Other containers also expose little to no configuration in the Unraid UI, requiring you to manually edit JSON config files to change basic settings like server name, password, or player cap. Every setting you need is exposed here as an environment variable you can set directly from the Unraid Docker UI.
FEATURES: - Auto-detects LAN IP for P2P proxy — works out of the box with no manual network config - Auto-updates server files on every start via SteamCMD — stays in sync with game patches automatically - First-launch config generation — ServerDescription.json and WorldDescription.json are created automatically, no manual file editing required - Full env var control — set invite code, server name, password, and max players from the Unraid UI - Password protection — leave SERVER_PASSWORD blank for a public server or set it to require a password - Persistent world saves — all save data lives in the mounted server-files volume - Clean shutdown handling — world state is flushed properly on container stop
SETUP: 1. Set INVITE_CODE to a string your crew will use under Play → Connect to Server in-game (1–32 characters) 2. Start the container — first boot takes a few minutes to download server files and generate config 3. Share your invite code — no port forwarding required
The game client and dedicated server versions must always match. The container auto-updates on start, but if a patch drops mid-session you will need to restart the container and update your game client.
Up to 4 players is recommended for best performance.

Config.Name: Server Files
Config.Description: Stores server binaries, config files (ServerDescription.json, WorldDescription.json), and world save data. Back this folder up before game updates.

Config.Name: Invite Code
Config.Description: The code players paste under Play → Connect to Server to find your server. 1–32 characters, alphanumeric (0-9, a-z, A-Z), case-sensitive. Share this with your crew.

Config.Name: Server Name
Config.Description: Display name shown in the server browser so players know they are joining the right place.

Config.Name: Server Password
Config.Description: Password required to join the server. Leave blank for a public server. If set, IsPasswordProtected is automatically enabled.

Config.Name: Max Players
Config.Description: Maximum simultaneous players. Up to 4 is strongly recommended for smooth performance. Exceeding this may impact stability, especially during late-game encounters. Hardware requirements scale with player count.

Config.Name: P2P Proxy Address
Config.Description: Leave blank (recommended). The server auto-detects the host LAN IP on startup and registers it with the Windrose backend so P2PGate can route player connections. Override only if auto-detection picks the wrong interface (e.g. on multi-NIC systems).

Config.Name: Update on Start
Config.Description: Download and validate server files via SteamCMD on every container start. Keeps the server in sync with game client updates automatically. Set to false to skip the update check (faster restarts, but you must update manually).

Config.Name: Generate Settings
Config.Description: Auto-generate ServerDescription.json and WorldDescription.json on first launch if they do not exist. Disable if you are supplying your own hand-crafted config files.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID the server process runs as. Unraid default is 99 (nobody). Match this to the owner of your appdata folder if you experience permission issues.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID the server process runs as. Unraid default is 100 (users). Match this to your appdata folder group if you experience permission issues.
